name: Pan-Sized Lemon-Blackberry Pancakes with Citr
recipeIngredient: Individual berries, not in a block
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together milk and lemon juice in a small nonreactive bowl and let stand 10 minutes.
2. In a large nonreactive bowl, mix together flour, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, salt, and lemon zest.
3. Whisk egg yolks separately.
4. Add the liquid mixture to the flour mixture and stir until just incorporated. Do not overmix; small lumps will remain.
5. In a clean bowl, whip egg whites until stiff but not dry.
6. Fold half of the egg whites into the batter to lighten it.
7. Gently fold in the remaining half of the egg whites.
8. Gently fold in the blackberries.
9. Preheat a 10-inch nonstick skillet over medium heat until hot (no water drops sizzle).
10. Ladle about 1 cup of batter into the pan, ensuring an even distribution of blackberries in each pancake.
11. Cook until puffed and golden brown with numerous bubbles on top, allowing the pancake enough time to set before flipping.
12. Flip the pancakes and cook on the other side until golden and fully cooked through in the center.
13. Serve immediately with a drizzle of warmed Citrus Syrup.
Citrus Syrup:
1 cup Water
2 cups Sugar
1/3 cup Fresh lemon juice
2 tbsp Grated lemon zest
1. In a heavy-bottomed nonreactive saucepan, combine water and sugar over medium heat.
2. Stir to dissolve the sugar completely.
3. Increase the heat to high once all the sugar has dissolved.
4. When the syrup comes to a boil, cover the pan and start timing immediately.
5. Boil the syrup for about 3 minutes.
6. Uncover the pan and add lemon juice and zest.
7. Continue boiling, uncovered, for an additional 3 minutes or until the syrup reaches a syrupy consistency.
8. If the syrup thickens during reheating, thin it with a little water.
9. If the syrup is too thin, boil for 1-2 more minutes to reduce.
